titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Arthur Lazarus Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday August 10, 2018 at 23:09
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, notes, newspaper clippings and printed briefs relating to Arthur Lazarus, his activism in the area of Native American civil rights, and his legal clients. The papers document Lazarus's legal work for two prominent cases: Tuscarora Indian Nation v. Power Authority of the State of New York, which came before the Supreme Court in 1958, and Montana v. Blackfeet Tribe, which came before the Supreme Court in 1985. Other clients and cases are also documented in the briefs, correspondence and clippings.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Arthur Lazarus Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Arthur Lazarus Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat WA MS 231
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and in some instances minimal organization, upon acquisition. Further description was added in 2011.

As a rule, descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section is drawn in large part from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Arthur Lazarus Jr., 1926-
bioghist.p: Arthur Lazarus is an attorney who specializes in American Indian Law. After graduating from Yale Law School in 1949, he worked with Felix Cohen in Washington D.C. until Cohen's death in 1953. Lazarus represented American Indian tribes in their claims against State and Federal governments, including several claims before the federal Indian Claims Commission. His clients included the Blackfeet Tribe, the Menominee Tribe, the Papago Tribe, the Seneca Nation, the Sioux Nation, and the Tuscarora Nation.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Business Papers, 1951-1987. II. Legal Briefs, 1947-1996.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of correspondence, notes, newspaper clippings and printed briefs relating to Arthur Lazarus, his activism in the area of Native American civil rights, and his legal clients. The papers document Lazarus' legal work for two prominent cases: Tuscarora Indian Nation v. Power Authority of the State of New York, which came before the Supreme Court in 1958, and Montana v. Blackfeet Tribe, which came before the Supreme Court in 1985. Other clients and cases are also documented in the briefs, correspondence and clippings.

subject.part: Indians of North America -- Government relations
Indians of North America -- Civil rights -- Montana.
Indians of North America -- Civil rights -- New York (State)
Indians of North America -- Legal status, laws, etc. -- Montana.
Indians of North America -- Legal status, laws, etc. -- New York (State)
Siksika Indians -- Legal status, laws, etc.
Tuscarora Indians -- Legal status, laws, etc.

corpname.part: United States ( Indian Civil Rights Act of 1968.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Business Papers consist chiefly of retained carbons of outgoing correspondence to clients and associates and newspaper clippings, documenting in particular Lazarus's work with three tribes: the Tuscarora Nation, the Blackfeet Tribe, and the Seneca Nation. Lazarus's work with other clients and cases is also documented in the correspondence and clippings.

did.unittitle: Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act land seminar
Articles by Felix Cohen, typescripts, printed versions, and related correspondence
Newspaper clippings relating to American Indian law
American Civil Liberties Union, Indian Civil Rights Committee, minutes and correspondence
Newspaper clippings relating to the Tuscarora Nation
Newspaper clippings relating to the Tuscarora Nation (continued)
Newspaper clippings relating to the Seneca Nation and Kinzua Dam

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Not arranged in any particular order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Legal briefs consist of printed versions of briefs authored by Arthur Lazarus and his associates, including Felix Cohen.

did.unittitle: AL, Jr. - Yale Law School, Indian Law seminar
AL, Jr. - Hualapai correspondence and legal papers
AL, Jr. - Nez Perce correspondence and legal papers
AL, Jr. - Salt River Pima-Maricopa correspondence
Title II of the 1968 Civil Rights Act: an Indian Bill of Rights
AL, Jr. - San Carlos Apache correspondence
